text: Each time 1 reed is placed on a non-empty "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space during the preparations phase, you receive 1 food. (Cost 1C.) Each time any player (including you) uses the "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you get 3 food, and each other player gets 1 food.

name: Drift-Net Boat
text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you receive an additional 2 food.

meta: (Cost 1W. Req play in round 7 or later.)
name: Throwing Axe
text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space while the "Take 1 Wild boar" accumulation space has at least 1 wild boar, you also receive 2 food. (Cost 1C.) In the returning home phase of each round, if you gained at least 7 building resources in the preceding work phase, you get 2 food.
rulings: Resources gained at the start of a round don't count.

name: Credit
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 5 food. At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you must pay 1 food, or else take a begging marker. (Cost 1W 1C.) Each time after you build an improvement, including this one, you get 1 food. (Cost 1R.) Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can exchange 2 wood for 3 food. If you do, place those 2 wood on the accumulation space.
rulings: You may take less than 2 wood from a wood accumulation space and still use this effect.
Food obtained from this effect may not be used to pay for Shifting Cultivator A091 .

meta: (1VP. Cost 2W. Req at least 4 unused farmyard spaces.)
name: Milking Parlor
text: When you play this card, if you have at least 1/3/4 sheep, you receive 2/3/4 food. The same applies for 1/2/3 cattle.

name: Asparagus Knife
text: In the returning home phases of rounds 8, 10, and 12, you can take 1 vegetable from exactly 1 vegetable field. You can immediately exchange it for 3 food and 1 bonus point.

name: Potato Ridger
text: Each time after you harvest at least 1 vegetable, if you then have at least 3/4 vegetables in your supply, you can/must exchange 1 vegetable for 6 food.
rulings: "Harvest" is equivalent to the field phase, or any literal effect of a card saying "Harvest a [crop/vegetable]".
(FotM) You can/must exchange 1 vegetable for 5 food.

name: Oriental Fireplace
text: At any time, you can turn goods into food. You receive 4 food per vegetable, 3 food per sheep, and 5 food per cattle. Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can turn grain into 2 food each.
rulings: This card counts as either 1 minor improvement or 1 major improvement, whichever is most convenient when considering another effect. But it never counts as 2 improvements at once.
If returned to pay for a Cooking Hearth A3/A4 , remove this card from the game.
You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock C053 .

name: Winnowing Fan
text: After the field phase of each harvest, you can bake exactly 1 grain. (This is not considered a "Bake Bread" action.)

name: Beer Keg
text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to exchange 1/2/3 grain for 0/1/2 bonus points and exactly 3 food.
rulings: (FotM) Up to a maximum of 6 bonus points. (2VP. Cost 2W 2S.) Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action in a round immediately following a harvest, you get 3 additional food.

name: Barley Mill
text: In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for each grain field that you harvest.

name: Seed Pellets
text: Each time before you take a "Sow" action, you receive 1 grain.

name: Feeding Dish
text: Each time you use an animal accumulation space while already having an animal of that type, you receive 1 grain. (Cost 1W.) Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you get 1 additional grain.

name: Asparagus Gift
text: Each time you build a number of fences equal to or greater than the current round, you immediately get 1 vegetable. (Cost 2W. Req 2 occ.) Add 4, 7, and 9 to the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.

name: Lifting Machine
text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can move 1 vegetable from one of your fields to your supply. (This is not considered a field phase.) (Cost 1W.) At any time, you can move 1 crop from a planted field containing at least 2 crops to an empty field.
rulings: (FotM) Usable one time per round.

name: Calcium Fertilizers
text: Each time you use a stone accumulation space, add 1 additional good of the respective type to each of your planted fields growing a single type of crop.

name: Agricultural Fertilizers
text: Each time after you turn at least 2 unused spaces into used spaces in one action, you get an additional "Sow" action.
rulings: One action means you only placed one person.

name: Stable Tree
text: Each time you build at least 1 stable on your turn, place 1 wood on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the wood.
rulings: Stables built off-turn, e.g. with Stable Planner A089 or Groom B089 , do not trigger this card. (2VP. Cost 2S. Req at most 3 occ.) Every improvement costs you 1 wood less.

name: Cob
text: At the start of each work phase, you can exchange exactly 1 clay plus 1 grain for 3 clay plus 1 food.

name: Hod
text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 1 clay. Each time any player (including you) uses the "Take 1 Wild boar" accumulation space, you immediately receive 2 clay. (1VP. Cost 2W. Req 1 occ.) Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get an additional 1 food and 1 reed.

name: Garden Hoe
text: Each time you take a "Sow" action planting vegetables in at least 1 field, you receive 1 clay and 1 stone. (Cost 1W.) Each time you use a stone accumulation space, you get 1 additional stone.

name: Interim Storage
text: Each time you use a clay/reed/stone accumulation space, place 1 wood/clay/reed on this card. At the start of rounds 7, 11, and 14, move all the goods on this card to your supply.

name: Work Certificate
text: Each time after you use an action space, you can take 1 building resource from a building resource accumulation space with at least 4 building resources on it.
rulings: This card's effect may not be immediately triggered. (Cost 1W.) Each time you fence a new pasture covering at least 4 farmyard spaces, you immediately get 2 sheep on this pasture.
Building 2 such pastures at once gives you 4 sheep.

name: Silage
text: In each returning home phase after which there is no harvest, you can pay exactly 1 grain - even from a field - to breed exactly one type of animal.

name: Upscale Lifestyle
text: You immediately get 5 clay and a "Renovation" action. If you take the action, you must pay the renovation cost. (Cost 2F.) Immediately fence a farmyard space, without paying wood for the fences. (If you already have pastures, the new one must be adjacent to an existing one.)

name: Moonshine
text: Randomly select an occupation in your hand. Either play it for an occupation cost of 2 food, or give it to the player to your left.
rulings: If you cannot pay to play an occupation and/or its additional cost, that occupation must be passed left (or removed from play in a solo game).
May not be played with no occupations in hand.

name: Wood Pile
text: You immediately get a number of wood equal to the number of people you have on accumulation spaces.

name: Store of Experience
text: If you have 0-4/5/6/7 occupations left in hand, you immediately get 1 stone/reed/clay/wood.

name: Excursion to the Quarry
text: You immediately get a number of stone equal to the number of people you have.
rulings: A newborn is a person.

name: Wage
text: You immediately get 2 food and 1 additional food for each major improvement you have from the bottom row of the supply board.
rulings: (FotM) Including each FotM major improvement from the bottom row of the supply board. (Cost 1 Grain.) You immediately get 1 vegetable. (Effectively, you are exchanging 1 grain for 1 vegetable.)

name: Beating Rod
text: You can immediately choose to either get 1 reed or exchange 1 reed for 1 cattle. (Cost 3W 3F.) This card provides room for 1 person.
rulings: The Caravan is not a room.

name: Feedyard
text: This card can hold 1 animal for each pasture you have, even different types. After the breeding phase of each harvest, you receive 1 food for each unused spot on this card.

name: Stockyard
text: This card can hold up to 3 animals of the same type. (It is not considered a pasture.) (Cost 1W 1S.) Wooden rooms only cost you 2 wood and 2 reed each.

meta: (Cost 2C. Req play in round 7 or before.)
name: Hawktower
text: Place a stone room on round space 12. If you live in a stone house at the start of that round, you can build the stone room at no cost. Otherwise, discard the stone room.

name: Carpenter's Bench
text: Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can use the taken wood (and only that) to build exactly 1 pasture. If you do, one of the fences is free.
rulings: Subdividing a pasture does not qualify as building. (Cost 1W.) When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. Each time you renovate, you can also build a stable without paying wood.

name: Forest Plow
text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can pay 2 wood to plow 1 field. Place the paid wood on the accumulation space (for the next visitor).
rulings: You may take less than 2 wood from the space and still use this card's effect.

meta: (Cost 2W. Req 2 occ 1 building resource in your supply.)
name: Grassland Harrow
text: Add 1 to the current round for each building resource in your supply and place 1 field on the corresponding round space. At the start of that round, you can plow the field. (Cost 2W. Req 1 occ.) Place 2 field tiles on this card. Twice this game, when you use the"Farmland" action space, you can also plow 1 field from this card.
prereq: 2 occ 1 building resource in your supply

name: Chain Float
text: Add 7, 8, and 9 to the current round and place 1 field on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plow the field.

name: Hayloft Barn
text: Place 4 food on this card. Each time you obtain at least 1 grain, you also get 1 food from this card. Once it is empty, you get a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.
rulings: Harvesting 2+ grain at once only counts as obtaining once, regardless of any other crops harvested.
(FotM) Once per turn, you obtain at least 1 grain, you also get 1 food from this card. From round 5, once it is empty, you get a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.

name: Walking Boots
text: You immediately get 2 food. You must immediately place a person from your supply. If you do, in the next returning home phase, you must remove that person from play.
rulings: Consequently, you may never grow to 5 people.
ERRATA:This card must be played in the work phase.
ERRATA:Remove the "If you do" clause.

name: Final Scenario
text: Place the action space card for round 14 face up in front of you. Only you can use it until it is placed on the game board. (Cost 1R.) You can place exactly two people immediately after one another if at least one of them uses the "Sheep Market", "Pig Market", or "Cattle Market" accumulation space. (Cost 1W.) When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each occupation you play, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.
rulings: Note the errata for Freshman A097 .

name: Agrarian Fences
text: Each time you use the "Sow and/or Bake Bread" action space, you can take a "Build Fences" action instead of one of the two actions provided by the action space.
rulings: You can take a "Build Fences" action in this way without the ability to sow or bake.

name: Toolbox
text: In the work phase, after each turn in which you build at least 1 room, stable, or fence, you can build the "Joinery", "Pottery", or "Basketmaker's Workshop" major improvement.
rulings: Stables built off-turn, e.g. with Stable Planner A089 or Groom B089 , do not trigger this card.
Pay the cost of the major improvement normally.

name: Forestry Studies
text: Each time after you use the "Take 3 Wood" accumulation space, you can return 2 wood to that space to play 1 occupation without paying an occupation cost.

name: Cookery Lesson
text: Each time you use an "Occupation" action space and a Cooking improvement on the same turn, you get 1 bonus point.
rulings: Cooking improvements have the bowl icon.
(FotM) Up to a maximum of 3 bonus points.

name: Wood Palisades
text: Instead of a fence piece, you can place 2 wood from your supply on fence spaces at the edge of your farmyard. These fence spaces with 2 wood are worth 1 bonus point.
rulings: The spaces with 2 wood (palisades) function as fences. Therefore, functionally, you may have more than 15 fences by building palisades and fences.
(FotM) Up to a maximum of 4 bonus points.

name: Pottery Yard
text: During scoring, if there at least 2 orthogonally adjacent unused spaces in your farmyard, you get 2 bonus points. (You still get the negative points for those unused spaces.)

name: Kettle
text: At any time, you can exchange 1/3/5 grain for 3/4/5 food plus 0/1/2 bonus points.
rulings: (FotM) Up to a maximum of 7 bonus points. (-3VP. Cost 1S. Req clay or stone house.) When you play this card, you immediately get 1 bonus point for each complete round left to play. You may no longer renovate your house.

name: Special Food
text: The next time you take animals from an accumulation space and accommodate all of them on your farm, you get 1 bonus point for each of these animals.
rulings: Taking, but not fully accommodating, the animals neither triggers nor voids the effect.
It's legal to avoid accommodating all animals even if you can, e.g. by eating them with a Fireplace.

name: Hook Knife
text: Once this game, when you have 9/8/7/6/5/5 sheep on your farm in a 1/2/3/4/5/6 player game, you immediately get 2 bonus points. (4VP. See below for cost.) For each person you have, you must pay an additional 1 clay and 1 food to play this card.
rulings: A newborn is a person.

meta: (3VP. Req 6 field tiles and all animal types.)
name: Grange
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food.
prereq: 6 field tiles and all animal types

meta: (3VP. Req play in round 4 or before.)
name: Future Building Site
text: Until all other farmyard spaces are used, you cannot use spaces that are orthogonally adjacent to your house.
rulings: You may use the adjacent spaces if you are using all the remaining other spaces in the same action.

(FotM) You cannot use a farmyard space that contains a moor tile or a forest tile that is orthogonally adjacent to your house. (1VP. Cost 2W. Req 2 occ.) In the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1/4/7 sheep, you get 1/2/3 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for every 3 sheep.

name: Brewery Pond
text: Each time you use the "Fishing" or "Take 1 Reed" accumulation space, you also get 1 grain and 1 wood.

name: Hauberg
text: Alternate placing 2 wood and 1 wild boar on the next 4 round spaces. You decide what to start with. At the start of these rounds, you get the goods.

meta: (1VP. Cost 1C 1R. Req play in round 6 or before.)
name: Forest Inn
text: This is an action space for all. A player who uses it can exchange 5/7/9 wood for 8 wood and 2/4/7 food. When another player uses it, they must first pay you 1 food.

name: Chophouse
text: Each time you use the "Take 1 Grain/Vegetable" action space, place 1 food on each of the next 3/2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
rulings: Note the errata for Swagman A129 .

name: Chick Stable
text: Add 3 and 4 to the current round and place 2 food on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (2VP. Cost 1W. Req 2 vegetable fields.) Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Club House
text: Place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces and 1 stone on the round space after that. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good. (Cost 1C.) Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Forest Stone
text: Place 2 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, move 1 of these food to your supply. Each time you use a stone accumulation space, add 2 food to this card.

name: Scales
text: Each time after you place an improvement or occupation in front of you, if you have the same number of improvements and occupations in play, you get 2 food.
rulings: Passing cards will not trigger this effect, as they are never in front of you. (1VP. Cost 1W. Req at most 3 occ.) In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for every 3 sheep and 1 food for every 2 cattle you have.

meta: (Cost 1W. Req play in round 7 or later.)
name: Digging Spate
text: Each time you use a clay accumulation space, you also get a number of food equal to the number of wild boar in your farmyard.

name: Growing Farm
text: You can only play this card if you have at least as many pasture spaces as the number of completed rounds. If you do, you get a number of food equal to the current round.

name: Sculpture Course
text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can use this card to exchange your choice of 1 wood for 2 food, or 1 stone for 4 food.

name: Tumbrel
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time after you take a "Sow" action, you get 1 food for each stable you have.

name: Maintenance Premium
text: Place 3 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 food from this card. Each time you renovate, restock this card to 3 food. (Req 1 of your people on "Fishing".) Each time you use one of the four action spaces above the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get 1 additional food. (Cost 1W 1C.) At the start of each round, if you live in a wooden house, you get 1 food.
rulings: (FotM) 1VP.

name: Crack Weeder
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each vegetable you take from a field in the field phase of a harvest, you also get 1 food.

name: Food Chest
text: If you play this card on the "Major Improvement" action space, you immediately get 4 food. Otherwise, you get only 2 food.

name: Brewing Water
text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can pay 1 grain to place 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
rulings: (FotM) 3 times during the game, when you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can pay 1 grain to place 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces. (Req 3 occ.) At the start of the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1 grain field, 1 vegetable field, and 1 empty field, you get 3 food. (Cost 1W.) Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, if the "Farmland" action space is occupied you also get 3 food.

name: Tasting
text: Each time you use an "Occupation" action space, before paying the occupation cost, you can exchange 1 grain for 4 food.

name: Mill Wheel
text: Each time you use the "Sow and/or Bake Bread" action space while the "Fishing" accumulation space is occupied, you get an additional 2 food.

name: Grain Depot
text: If you paid wood/clay/stone for this card, place 1 grain on each of the next 2/3/4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain. (Req 2 occ. Cost 2W.) Place 1 grain each on the remaining spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11, and 14. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.

name: Hand Truck
text: Each time before you take a "Bake Bread" action, you also get 1 grain for each of your people occupying an accumulation space.
rulings: (FotM) Once per turn, whenever you take a "Bake Bread" action, you also get 1 grain if 1 of your people occupying an accumulation space. (1VP. Req 2 occ. Cost 1F.) This card is a field that can only grow vegetables.

name: Potters Market
text: At any time, you can pay 3 clay and 2 food. If you do, place 1 vegetable on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.

name: New Purchase
text: Before the start of each round that ends with a harvest, you can buy one of each of the following crops: 1 grain for 2 food 1 vegetable for 4 food

name: Harvest House
text: When you play this card, if the number of completed harvests is equal to the number of occupations you played, you immediately get 1 food, 1 grain and 1 vegetable.

name: Love for Agriculture
text: You can sow crops in pastures covering 1 or 2 farmyard spaces. If you do, these pastures are also considered fields and hold 1 and 2 animals less, respectively.

name: Gift Basket
text: When you play this card, if you have exactly 2/3/4/5 rooms, you immediately get 1 vegetable/food/grain/vegetable. (Req 5 occ in your supply.) Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.

name: Wood Workshop
text: Each time before you play or build an improvement, you get 1 wood.
rulings: You are able to pay for the improvement with just the wood given by this card.

name: Ceilings
text: Place 1 wood on next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood. Remove the wood promised by this card from future round spaces the next time you renovate. (1VP. Req 3 occ. Cost 1F.) Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you also get 3 clay.
rulings: These clay may be spent to pay for the effect of the Cottager B087 .

name: Reed Belt
text: Place 1 reed on each remaining space for rounds 5, 8, 10, and 12. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.

name: Corf
text: Each time any player (including you) takes at least 3 stone from an accumulation space, you get 1 stone from the general supply. (Cost 1C.) At any time, you can exchange 2/3/4 clay for 1/2/3 stone.
rulings: (FotM) You can exchange 2/3 clay for 1/2 stone.

name: Handcart
text: Before the start of each work phase, you can take 1 building resource from at most one wood/clay/reed/stone accumulation space containing at least 6/5/4/4 building resources of the same type.

name: Value Assets
text: After each harvest, you can buy exactly one of the following building resources: 1 wood for 1 food 1 clay for 1 food 1 reed for 2 food 1 stone for 2 food

name: Reed Belt
text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 wild boar, 1 food, 1 cattle, 1 food, and 1 sheep on this card. At any time, you can pay 1 clay to take the top good. (Req 3 occ. Cost 1R.) Place 1 wild boar on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wild boar.

name: Overhaul
text: Immediately raze all of your fences, add up to 3 fences from your supply, and rebuild them. (You do not lose any animals during this.)
rulings: For purposes of combos, you are building fences. (Cost 1W.) Immediately build 1 stable. (The stable costs you nothing, but you must pay the cost shown on this card.) (Req 1 person yet to place.) If you play this card in the work phase, you can immediately place another person.

name: Writing Boards
text: You immediately get 1 wood for each occupation you have in front of you.

name: Remodeling
text: You immediately get 1 clay for each clay room and for each major improvement you have. (Cost 1F.) Immediately place 1 stone on each of your empty fields. Harvest them during the next field phase. These fields are considered planted until then.
rulings: ERRATA: harvest the fields with stone normally, and the fields are considered planted until the stone is gone.
(FotM) Up to a maximum of 2 stone.

name: Blade Shears
text: You immediately get your choice of 3 food or 1 food for each sheep you have. (Keep the sheep.)
rulings: Choose exactly 3 food, or food equal to your sheep.

name: Plant Fertilizer
text: In each field with exactly 1 good, you can immediately place 1 additional good of the same type.
rulings: Boar held on unplanted fields (from Mud Patch A011 ) do not apply for this effect.
If there is exactly 1 wood on the Wood Field D075 , another wood may be added on either the same or a different stack.

name: Automatic Water Trough
text: If you can accommodate the animal, you can immediately buy 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food.

name: Bunk Beds
text: Once you have 4 rooms, your house can hold 5 people. (1VP. Cost 2W. Req 2 occ.) This card can hold up to 1 sheep, 1 wild boar, and 1 cattle.
rulings: This card does not count as a pasture.

name: Cattle Farm
text: For each pasture you have, you can keep 1 cattle on this card.

name: Wood Slide Hammer
text: On your first renovation, if you have at least 5 wood rooms, you can renovate to stone directly and you get a discount of 2 stone on the renovation cost. (1VP. Req 3 grain fields.) You no longer need reed to renovate or build a room.

name: Trellis
text: Each time before you use the "Take 1 Wild Boar" accumulation space, you can take a "Build Fences" action. (You must pay wood for the fences as usual.) (Cost 2F.) You can immediately take a "Build Fences" action, during which you do not have to pay wood for fences that you build next to field tiles.

name: Newly Plowed Field
text: When you play this card, you can immediately plow 1 field, which needs not be adjacent to another field.

name: Roll-Over Plow
text: At any time, if you have at least 3 planted fields, you can discard all goods from one of those fields to plow 1 field. (Cost 3W. Req 3 occ.) Place 4 field tiles on this card. Each time you use the "Farmland" action space, you can also plow up to 2 fields from this card.

meta: (Cost 3W 1F. Req play in round 9 or later.)
name: Mole Plow
text: Each time you use the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow 1 Field and/or Sow" action space, you can plow 1 additional field.

name: Heart of Stone
text: Each time a Stone accumulation space is revealed, if you have room in your house, you can immediately take a "Family Growth" action without placing a person.

name: Basket Chair
text: When you play this card, you can immediately move the first person you placed this work phase to this card. If you do, immediately afterward, you can place another person.
rulings: If the first person placed added a newborn (usually via an Family Growth action), the newborn also moves to this card.

name: Job Contract
text: If both are unoccupied, you can use the "Day Laborer" and the adjacent "Occupation" action space with a single person (in that order). Afterward, both spaces are considered occupied.
rulings: Recommendation: occupy Day Laborer with a suggestion marker and Lessons with your person.

name: Bed in the Grain Field
text: At the start of the next harvest, you get a "Family Growth" action if you have room for the newborn.
rulings: The newborn must be fed. (1VP. Cost 2W.) Each work phase, if the last action space you use is an accumulation space, you can immediately afterward take a "Bake Bread" action. (Cost 1W.) When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you can also take a "Bake Bread" action. (Cost 1F.) You can build the major improvements "Joinery", "Pottery", and "Basketmaker's Workshop" even when taking a "Minor Improvement" action. They each cost you 1 stone less.

name: Teacher's Desk
text: Each time you use the "Major Improvement" or "Renovation and Improvement" action space, you can also play 1 occupation at an occupation cost of 1 food.

meta: (Cost 2W. Req no grain in your supply.)
name: Beer Table
text: At the end of the field phase of each harvest, you can pay 1 grain from your supply to get 2 bonus points. If you do, all other players get 1 food each. (Cost 1W 1C 2S 1R.) During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each stone room you have. You can only use one card to get bonus points for your stone house.

name: Writing Chamber
text: During scoring, you get a number of bonus points equal to the total of negative points you have, to a maximum of 7 bonus points.
rulings: Negative point examples: printed card values, beggars, negative categories, negative bonuses.

name: Abort Oriel
text: You can no longer play this card when any player (including you) has 5 or more cards in front of them.
rulings: This card may be played as one's fifth card.

name: Greening Plan
text: During scoring, if you then have at least 2/4/5/6 unplanted fields, you get 1/2/3/5 bonus points.
rulings: Garden Designer C099 allows planting of food (see errata) so such fields do not count.
Boar held on unplanted field tiles (from Mud Patch A011 ) do not affect scoring of this card.

name: Elephantgrass Plant
text: Immediately after each harvest, you can use this card to exchange exactly 1 reed for 1 bonus point.
rulings: (FotM) Up to a maximum of 5 bonus points. (7VP. Cost 1W. Req no occ.) During scoring, you get 1 negative point for each card left in your hand. You cannot discard cards from your hand unplayed. If you already have, you cannot play this card.

name: Clay Deposit
text: Immediately after each time you use a clay accumulation space, you can exchange 1 clay for 1 bonus point. If you do, place the clay on the accumulation space. (3VP. Cost 1F. Play in round 3 or before.) From now on, you must pay 1 food for each new field tile that you place in your farmyard.
rulings: You must be able to pay the food cost before placing the field tile.

name: Christianity
text: When you play this card, all other players get 1 food each.

name: Studio Boat
text: Each time you use the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you also get 1 bonus point. In games with 1-3 players, this card is considered "Traveling Players" (same effect as "Fishing").
rulings: In 1/2/3 player game, the card becomes another accumulation space (+1 food) for all.

meta: (1VP. Cost 1 grain or 1R. Req no occ.)
name: Canvas Sack
text: When play this card, paying grain/reed for it, you immediately get 1 vegetable/4 wood. (Cost 2W 2C.) After the feeding phase of each harvest, you can exchange exactly 1 food for 2 different building resources of your choice or 1 vegetable.
rulings: (FotM) 2 different building resources except stone. (Cost 1 Grain.) Immediately after each time you use the "Vegetable Seeds" action space, you can place another person on an accumulation space and get 1 additional good of the accumulating type.

name: Farm Building
text: Each time you build a major improvement, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (1VP. Cost 2W/2C 1R.) Place 1 food on each of the next 8 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Stew
text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, also place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Mandoline
text: Once per round, you can pay 1 vegetable to get 1 bonus point. If you do, place 1 food on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Garden Claw
text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space, up to three times the number of planted fields you have. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Farmstead
text: After each turn in which you make at least one unused farmyard space used, you get 1 food.

name: Beer Stall
text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, for each empty unfenced stable you have, you can exchange 1 grain for 5 food.

name: Stable Yard
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food for each complete round left to play. At any time, you can exchange 1 sheep plus 1 wild boar for 1 cattle. (1VP. Cost 1R.) Each time another player uses the "Fishing" accumulation space, they must first pay you 1 food. Then, in the returning home phase of that round, place 2 food on "Fishing".
rulings: Others must have 1 food before using "Fishing".
Food from the "Fishing" action space may not be used to pay the card owner.
The 2 food is only placed for rounds in which another player used "Fishing".

name: Huntsman's Hat
text: For each new wild boar you get from the effect of an action space, you also get 1 food.

name: Gypsy's Crock
text: Each time you use a cooking improvement to turn 2 goods into food at the same time, you get 1 additional food.

name: Market Stall
text: Remove 1 stable from your supply. After the field phase of each harvest, you can exchange 1 grain plus 1 fence (both from your supply) for 5 food.

name: Studio
text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 wood/clay/stone into 2/2/3 food. (Cost 1W.) For each new stable you build, you get 1 food - for your last one, get 3 food. Each time you build stables, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 clay instead of wood.
rulings: This card does not grant the ability to build additional stables, only to change any wood cost into 1 clay.

name: Crudite
text: When you play this card, you can immediately buy exactly 1 vegetable for 3 food. At any time, you can discard 1 vegetable on top of another vegetable in a field to get 4 food.
rulings: (FotM) Usable once per round and 5 times during the game.

name: Woodcraft
text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, if immediately afterward you have at most 5 wood in your supply, you get 1 food. (2VP. Cost 1S 1 vegetable.) In each feeding phase, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 vegetable into 5 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point each for your 5th and 6th vegetable.

meta: (5VP. Cost 2C. Req return the clay/stone oven.)
name: Small Potter's Oven
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 5 food. Each time before you get a "Bake Bread" action, you can build the "Clay Oven" or "Stone Oven" major improvement.
rulings: Is an oven, is not an upgrade, and cannot bake.

name: Beer Stein
text: Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can use this card once to turn 1 grain into 2 food and 1 bonus point.

name: Cooking Hearth Extension
text: Each harvest, you can use each of your cooking improvements once to get double the amount of food for 1 animal or vegetable.

name: Craft Brewery
text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to exchange 1 grain from your supply plus 1 grain from a field for 2 bonus points and 4 food.

name: Corn Schnapps Distillery
text: Once per round, you can pay 1 grain to place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (1VP. Cost 3W/3C.) Place 1 grain each on the remaining spaces for rounds 8, 10, and 12. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.

name: Eternal Rye Cultivation
text: After each harvest in which you have 2 or 3+ grain in your supply, you get 1 food and 1 additional grain, respectively.
rulings: ERRATA: last "and" should be "or".

name: Mineral Feeder
text: At the start of each round that does not end with a harvest, if you have at least 1 sheep in a pasture, you get 1 grain. (Cost 2W. Req 1 occ.) Each time after you play an occupation, you get 1 vegetable.

name: Land Consolidation
text: At any time, you can exchange 3 grain in a field for 1 vegetable in that field. (1VP. Req 3 occ.) This card is a field that can only grow vegetables. You can immediately turn each vegetable you harvested from this card into 4 food.

name: Slurry
text: In the breeding phase of each harvest, if you get newborn animals of at least two types, you also get a "Sow" action.

name: Harvest Festival Planning
text: When you play this card, immediately carry out the field phase of the harvest. Afterwards, you get a "Major or Minor Improvement" action.

name: Seaweed Fertilizer
text: Each time after you take an unconditional "Sow" action, you get 1 grain from the general supply. From round 11 on, you can take 1 vegetable instead. (Cost 2F. Req 1 occ.) Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.

name: Firewood
text: In the returning home phase of each round, place 1 wood on this card. Each time before you build a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth, or oven, move up to 4 wood from this card to your supply.

name: Wood Cart
text: Whenever you use a person to take Wood that is on an Action space, you receive 2 additional Wood.

name: Clay Supply
text: Place 1 clay on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the clay.

name: Toad
text: Add 5,7,9,11 and 13 to the current round and place 1 reed on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the reed. (Cost 2W. Req 2 occ.) Place 1 stone on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the stone.

name: Rocky Terrain
text: Each time you plow a field (tile or card), you can also buy 1 stone for 1 food.
rulings: Playing field cards counts as plowing a field.

name: Material Hub
text: Immediately place 2 of each building resource on this card. Each time any player (including you) takes at least 5 wood, 4 clay, 3 reed, or 3 stone, you get 1 of that building resource from this card.

meta: (1VP. Cost 1W 1C. Req 1 reed 1 stone in your supply.)
name: Hardware Store
text: Each time after you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can pay 2 food total to buy 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone.

name: Early Cattle
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 cattle.

name: Perennial Rye
text: Each round that does not end with a harvest, you can pay 1 grain to breed exactly 1 type of animal. (This is not considered a breeding phase.)

meta: (Cost 1W. Req 3 fields in an L shape.)
name: Zigzag Harrow
text: You can immediately plow 1 field such that is completes a "zigzag" pattern. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

name: Dwelling Plan
text: You can immediately take a "Renovation" action. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

name: Furrows
text: You can immediately sow in exactly 1 field. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

name: Cross-Cut Wood
text: You immediately get a number of wood equal to the number of stone in your supply. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

name: Field Clay
text: You immediately get 1 clay for each planted field you have. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

name: Petrified Wood
text: Immediately exhange up to 3 wood for 1 stone each. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand. (Cost 1W. Play in round 3, 6, 9 or 12.) If you play this card in round 3/6/9/12, you immediately get 3/4/5/6 food.

name: Fern Seeds
text: You get 2 food and 1 grain, which you must sow immediately. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand. (Cost 2 sheep.) You immediately get 1 wild boar and 1 cattle. (Effectively, you are exchanging 2 sheep for 1 wild boar and 1 cattle.) (Cost 1R. Req 5 occ.) In the returning home phase of each round, if you have more rooms than people, you can pay 1 food to take a "Family Growth" action.
rulings: To clarify, this can only be used once per round.
(FotM) Req 2 occ and usable from round 5.

name: Lawn Fertilizer
text: Your pastures of size 1 can hold up to 3 animals of the same type. (With a stable, they can hold up to 6 animals of the same type.) (1VP. Cost 1 Grain.) In the feeding phase of each harvest, you get 1 food. You can no longer hold animals in your house (not even via another card).

name: Trowel
text: At any time, you can renovate your house to stone. From a wooden house, this costs 1 stone, 1 reed and 1 food per room. From a clay house, this costs 1 stone per room.

name: Hammer Crusher
text: Immediately before you renovate to stone, you get 2 clay and 1 reed and you can take a "Build Rooms" action. (Cost 2W.) Each time you build a clay room, you can pay 2 clay, 1 wood, and 1 reed instead of 5 clay and 2 reed.

name: Wooden Whey Bucket
text: Each time before you use the "Sheep Market"/ "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood/ at no cost.

name: Drill Harrow
text: Each time before you take an unconditional "Sow" action, you can pay 3 food to plow 1 field.
rulings: (FotM) You can pay 2 food to plow 1 field.

name: Steam Plow
text: Immediately after each returning home phase, you can pay 2 wood and 1 food to use the "Farmland" action space without placing a person.

name: Pulverizer Plow
text: Immediately after each time you use a clay accumulation space, you can pay 1 clay to plow 1 field. If you do, place that clay on the accumulation space. (Cost 3W. Req 2 occ.) Place 2 field tiles on this card. Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you can also plow up to 2 fields from this card.

meta: (Cost 1F. Req No people left in the house.)
name: Recruitment
text: Provided you have room in your house, each time you get a "Minor Improvement" action, you can take a "Family Growth" action instead.
rulings: (FotM) From round 5, you can take a "Family Growth" action instead.
prereq: No people left in the house

meta: (Cost 1F. Req At least 1 building resource in your supply.)
name: Work Permit
text: Add 1 to the current round for each building resource you have and place 1 person from your supply on the corresponding round space. In that round, you can use the person.
prereq: At least 1 building resource in your supply

name: Pioneering Spirit
text: This card is an action space for you only. In rounds 3-5, it provides a "Renovation" action. In rounds 6-8, it provides your choice of 1 vegetable, wild boar or cattle.

name: Brotherly Love
text: As long as you have exactly 4 people, in the work phase of each round, you can place your third and fourth person immediately after one another, even on the same action space.

name: Witches Dance Floor
text: This card is a field that you can sow in, an occupation, and the "Fireplace" major improvement with all of its effects. You can play it only via a "Minor Improvement" action.
rulings: ERRATA: The card grants no actual Fireplace goods-to-food conversions, you can exchange it for a Cooking Hearth.

This card counts as either 1 field, 1 occupation, 1 minor improvement, or 1 major improvement, whichever is most convenient when considering another effect. But it never counts as multiple entities at once.

name: Carpenter's Yard
text: You can build the "Joinery" and "Well" major improvement even when taking a "Minor Improvement" action, or you can build both with a single "Major Improvement" action.

name: Retraining
text: At the end of each turn in which you renovate, you can exchange your Joinery for the Pottery or your Pottery for the Basketmaker's Workshop. (1VP. Cost 1W. Req 2 occ.) Each time you use a "Lessons" action space, you can play 1 additional occupation for an occupation cost of 2 food. (Cost 1W.) During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for exactly 1 unfenced stable holding exactly 1 sheep. The same applies to wild boar and cattle, if held in different unfenced stables.

name: Artisan District
text: During scoring, you get 2/5/8 bonus points for having 3/4/5 Major Improvements from the bottom row of the supply board.
rulings: (FotM) You get 2/3/4 bonus points for having 3/4/5 Major Improvements from the bottom row of the supply board (including FotM major improvements).

name: Storeroom
text: During scoring, you get 0,5 bonus point for each pair of grain plus vegetable you have (considering all crops in your supply and fields), rounded up.
rulings: (FotM) Up to a maximum of 3 bonus points.

name: Wood Rake
text: During scoring, if you had at least 7 goods in your fields before the final harvest, you get 2 bonus points.

name: Summer House
text: During scoring, if you live in a stone house, you get 2 bonus points for each unused farmyard space orthogonally adjacent to your house. (You still lose the points for these unused spaces.)
rulings: (FotM) Cost reduced to 1W 1S.

name: Luxurious Hostel
text: During scoring, if you then have more stone rooms than people, you get 4 bonus points. You can only use one card to bonus points for your stone house.

name: Fodder Chamber
text: During scoring, in a game with 1/2/3/4+ players, you get 1 bonus point for every 7th/5th/4th/3rd animal on your farm.
rulings: (FotM) Cost reduced to 2 Grain 2 Stone. For every 7th/5th/4th/3rd animal (excluding horse) on your farm.

name: Breed Registry
text: During scoring, if you gained at most 2 sheep from sources other than breeding during the game and have not turned any sheep into food, you get 3 bonus points.
rulings: (FotM) You get 2 bonus points. (2VP. Cost 1S. See below for req.) You can only play this card if there are more complete rounds left to play than you have unused farmyard spaces.

(FotM) You can play this card from round 7. (Cost 1W. Req 2 occ.) In the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1/3/5 cattle, you get 1/2/3 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for every 2 cattle you have. (Cost 1W. Play in round 8 or later.) Each time you use a wood accumulation space, if you have at least 1 wild boar, you can pay 1 food for 1 bonus point.
(FotM) Up to a maximum of 3 bonus points.

name: Cesspit
text: Alternate placing 1 clay and 1 wild boar on each remaining round space, starting with clay. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good.
rulings: (FotM) Req 3 fields and 1 occ. (Cost 2W. Req 3 occ.) Alternate placing 1 food and 1 sheep on each remaining round space, starting with food. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good. (Cost 1F. Req 2 imp.) After you play your 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th/6th occupation this game, you immediately get 1 grain/clay/reed/stone/vegetable/field (not retroactively). (1VP. Cost 1W 1R.) Place 0, 1, 2, and 3 food in this order on the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Forest Well
text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space, up to the amount of wood in your supply. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (2VP. Cost 2S.) Place 1 food on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of sheep you have. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

name: Pellet Press
text: Once per round, you can pay 1 reed. If you do, place 1 food on each of the next 4 rounds spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
rulings: (FotM) Up to a maximum of 4 reed.

name: Churchyard
text: Place 2 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (*Occupations and Improvements)
rulings: Cards must be Occupations and Improvements.

name: Civic Facade
text: Before the start of each round, if you have more occupations than improvements in your hand, you get 1 food.
rulings: (FotM) 1 VP. (1VP. Cost 1W. Req 3 occ.) Immediately before each time you play an occupation (even before paying the occupation cost), you get 3 food. (Play in round 11 or before.) When you play this card, you immediately get 6 food. You may no longer use the action spaces of rounds 12 to 14.
(FotM) 2 VP and you get 8 PN.

name: Archway
text: This card is an action space for all. A player who uses it immediately gets 1 food. Immediately before the returning home phase, they can use an unoccupied action space with the person from this card.
rulings: That Action ends the Work phase.
(FotM) 3 VP.

name: Rolling Pin
text: In the returning home phase of each round, if you have more clay than wood in your supply, you get 1 food.

meta: (2VP. Cost 1W 1S. Req Play in round 6 or later.)
name: Tea House
text: Once per round, you can skip placing your second person and get 1 food instead. (You can place the person later that round.)
rulings: (FotM) You may not do this if you will be placing more people after your second without another player placing in between.

name: Trout Pool
text: At the start of each work phase, if there are at least 3 food on the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get 1 food from the general supply.

name: New Market
text: Each time you use an action space card on round spaces 8 to 11, you get 1 additional food.

name: Fatstock Stretcher
text: Each time you turn a sheep or wild boar into food using 1 cooking improvement, you get 1 additional food.

name: Wholesale Market
text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.

meta: (Cost 1W. Req Play in round 5 or later.)
name: Gritter
text: At the end of each action in which you sow vegetables in a field, you get 1 food for each vegetable field you have (including the new ones).

name: Earth Oven
text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetable → 3 food; sheep → 2 food; wild boar → 3 food; cattle → 3 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain → 2 food.
rulings: This card counts as either1 minor improvement or 1 major improvement, whichever is most convenient when considering another effect. But it never counts as 2 improvements at once.
You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock C053 .

name: Large Pottery
text: At any time, you may convert 1 clay to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3/4 bonus points for 3/5/6/7 clay.

name: Bale of Straw
text: At the start of each harvest, if you have at least 3 grain fields (including field cards with planted grain), you get 2 food.

name: Beer Tap
text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can turn 2/3/4 grain into 3/6/9 food.

name: Lynchet
text: In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for each harvested field tile that is orthogonally adjacent to your house.

name: Baking Course
text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can take a "Bake Bread" action (you may convert 1 grain into 2 food).

name: Grain Sieve
text: In the field phase of each harvest, if you harvest at least 2 grain, you get 1 additional grain from the general supply.) Each time before you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can exchange exactly 1 clay for 1 grain.

name: Reap Hook
text: Place 1 grain on each of the next 3 of the round spaces 4, 7, 9, 11, 13, and 14. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.

name: Small Basket
text: Each time you use the "Reed Bank" accumulation space, you can pay 1 reed to get 1 vegetable. If you do in a game with 4+ players, place that 1 reed on the accumulation space. (1VP. Cost 2W. Req 1 occ.) Add 4 and 7 to the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can buy the vegetable for 1 food.

name: Straw Manure
text: Before the field phase of each harvest, you can pay 1 grain from your supply to add 1 vegetable to each of up to 2 vegetable fields.

name: Changeover
text: At any time, if a field contains exactly 1 good as a result of a harvest, you can discard that good and immediately take a "Sow" action limited to that field.

name: Stable Manure
text: In the field phase of each harvest, you can harvest 1 additional good from a number of fields equal to the number of unfenced stables you have. (1VP. Cost 1W. Req 1 occ.) Each time after you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can choose to buy 1 grain for 1 food, or 1 vegetable for 3 food.

name: Royal Wood
text: At the end of each turn in which you use the "Farm Expansion" action space or build an improvement, you get 1 wood back for every 2 wood paid during those actions (rounded down). (1VP. Cost 1F. Req 1 occ.) You can plant wood on this card as though it were 2 fields, but it is considered 1 field. Sow and harvest wood on this card as you would grain.
rulings: ERRATA: add "You can plant only wood on this card."
You may plant 2 wood at once with 1 trigger of the Chief Forester A115 .
Planted wood may not be spent during scoring for the Joinery 8 / 18 .

name: Social Benefits
text: Immediately after the feeding phase of each harvest, if you have no food left, you get 1 wood and 1 clay.

name: Recycled Brick
text: Each time any player (including you) renovate to stone, you get 1 clay for each newly renovated room. (Req 3 occ.) Place 1 reed on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed. (2VP. Cost 1W 2C. Play in round 8 or before.) At the end of rounds 8, 10, and 12, you get 1 stone for each vegetable field you have and a number of wood equal to the number of vegetables in your supply.
rulings: (FotM) Play in round 7 or before and up to a maximum of 2 stone and 2 wood at the end of rounds 8, 10 and 12.

name: Brick Hammer
text: Each time after you build an improvement costing at least 2 clay, you get 1 stone.

name: Roof Ladder
text: Each time you renovate, you pay 1 fewer reed and, at the end of the action, you get 1 stone.

name: Hunting Trophy
text: Improvements built on "House Redevelopment" cost you 1 building resource of your choice less. Fences built on "Farm Redevelopment" cost you a total of 3 wood less.
rulings: Alone, "built" can refer to either improvement type.

name: Pigswill
text: Each time you use the "Fencing" action space, you also get 1 wild boar. When you play this card, you immediately get 1 sheep. In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can exchange exactly 1 vegetable for 1 animal of a type you already have.

name: Land Agent
text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Vegetable" action space, you also receive 1 grain. When you play this card, you receive 1 vegetable from the general supply.

name: Academic
text: This card counts as 2 occupations for minor improvements and when scoring the Reeve E217 or Tutor E174 occupation card.
rulings: Playing the Academic counts as 1 occupation for the Bread Paddle K111 and Bookshelf K112 .

name: Master Baker
text: Whenever another player bakes bread, you can bake bread if you have a baking improvement with the "baking" symbol. If you take a bake action yourself, you receive 1 additional food.
rulings: Is activated when another player uses a baking improvement (with the "baking" symbol) to convert grain to food.
Is not activated if another player uses the "Sow and Bake Bread" action space, but does not bake.
Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill I103 , Windmill E17 or Hand Mill I75 is used.
You do not receive the additional food if you bake bread because of the Master Baker itself.

name: Baker
text: During each harvest, you may bake bread at the start of the feeding phase if you have an appropriate improvement. When you play this card, you may bake bread as an additional action.
rulings: You may only use the Baker to bake bread just once each harvest, but you may use all of your ovens and other improvements to bake as much bread as you can.

name: Master Builder
text: Once during the game, at any time after your home reaches at least 5 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.
rulings: You may place a room tile on this card to show that you have not yet taken the extension action.
You do not have to use an action space to build a room with the Master Builder.

name: Berry Picker
text: Whenever you use a person's action to take wood, you receive an additional 1 food.
rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which wood is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no wood (e.g. because of the Wood Distributor K284 ).
Is activated even if you leave all the wood on the action space because of Basket E34 , Mushroom Collector E196 , or Pig Catcher I253 .
Is activated when you use an action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game, and the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take wood.
Is activated when you play Building Material E16 . Is not activated when you receive wood because of any other minor improvement or an occupation.

name: Mendicant
text: At the end of the game, you can discard up to 2 begging cards without losing points for them.

name: Master Brewer
text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, the Master Brewer can convert up to 1 grain to 3 food.
rulings: Brewing beer does not count as baking.
The grain that you want to convert into 3 food may not be on fields; it must be taken from your personal supply.

name: Bread Seller
text: You receive 1 food from the general supply for each grain that is baked whenever any player (including yourself) bakes bread.
rulings: Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill I103 , Windmill E17 or Hand Mill I75 is used.
Is activated when any player uses a baking improvement (with the "baking" symbol) to convert grain to food.
Is not activated if anybody uses the "Sow and Bake Bread" action space, but does not bake.

name: Brush Maker
text: Whenever you convert wild boar to food, you place the boar on this card. At the end of the game, you receive bonus points if you have wild boar markers on this card: 4 or more markers, 3 points; 3 markers, 2 points; 2 markers, 1 point.
rulings: A converted wild boar may either be placed on the Tanner K280 , Taxidermist Z330 , or Brush Maker; you cannot use two of these cards for the same animal.
If you also have the Basin Maker K273 and/or the Pelts K339 , you may use those cards as well as the Brush Maker for each converted wild boar.
Wild boar markers on this card do not count in the scoring at the end of the game.

name: Thatcher
text: Pay 1 reed less to build each room, for each renovation, and for each of the Water Mill I103 , Half-timbered House E21 , Chicken Coop I84 , Holiday House I71 , Mansion K144 and Corn Storehouse I86 .
rulings: Can be used for every room you build, if you build more than 1 room.
If you also have other cards that reduce costs for building rooms, e.g. the Ladder I91 , you may use all of these cards for the same action.
The Thatcher has no effect if the number of reeds required is already lowered to 0, e.g. by using both Clay Roof E36 and Frame Builder K272 when building a new room.

name: Turner
text: At any time, you can use the Turner to convert any number of wood to 1 food each.

name: Head of the Family
text: You can use any room building or family growth action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.
rulings: You may not use the same action space in the same round with 2 of your own people.

If the action space provides a choice of actions, you may choose either. For example, you may take the "Traveling Players" action on the "Build 1 Room or Traveling Players" action space in the 5-player game, or play an occupation on the "1 Occupation or Family Growth" action space.
It is not allowed to use an empty accumulating action such as "Traveling Players", not even if it activates other improvements or occupations.

name: Farmer
text: The next time you build fences, you receive 1 wild boar. Each time you build at least 1 fence after that, you receive 1 cattle.
rulings: You only receive 1 new animal per turn, even if several pastures are created with the same action.
Is activated when you use the Fence Overseer K312 , or when you play the Mini Pasture E40 .
You may immediately convert the animals into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

name: Fisherman
text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" action space, you can choose to take twice as many food as is on the space. If you do this, you must give 1 food each to the owners of the Fishing Rod E12 , Raft E22 , Canoe E30 , Fish Trap I95 , and Landing Net K126 .
rulings: Only the food that is lying on the action space is doubled. Take the food from the action space, and the same amount from the general supply.
You only give food to another player if he has the improvement in front of him on the table.
If a player has played more than one of these improvements, you give him more than one food.
You do not have to pay anything for an item if you own it yourself.
It is possible that you have to pay more food than you receive. If you won't have enough food to pay the owners of the improvements after using the Fisherman, you are not allowed to use it.
The effect of the Fisherman is optional. If you do not take the double amount of food, you do not have to pay anything to the other players.
Is not activated by using the Net Fisherman I248 .

name: Meat Seller
text: If you have an oven, you can convert your animals into food at any time. Take 2 food for each sheep, 3 for each wild boar and 4 for each cattle.
rulings: The Meat Seller may not be used after upgrading an oven to a Baker's Kitchen I65 or Bakehouse K106 , unless you have another oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.
Ovens are not a cooking improvement, even if you have the Meat Seller. Using the Meat Seller does ¨ not activate the Schnitzel Mallet O18 .

name: Field Warden
text: You can use the "Take 1 Vegetable", "Plough 1 Field" and "Plough Field and Sow" action spaces even if another player has already placed a person on the space.
rulings: You may not use the same action space in the same round with 2 of your own people.
You can use the Field Warden when you move a family member to the "Plough Field and Sow" space because of the Countryman K289 .

name: Master Forester
text: Include the "2 Wood" action card from the 3-player game as an additional forest. At the start of each round, place 2 wood on the card. Any player who uses this action space must pay you 2 food.
rulings: If you use the Master Forester yourself, you do not need to have or to pay any food.

The food must be paid before the wood is collected. If the player who wants the wood gets food for this action, for example because of the Berry Picker E152 , he may not use this food to pay for the Master Forester.

name: Yeoman Farmer
text: At the end of the game, you only lose points for unused farmyard spaces and begging cards.
rulings: Write down the points you gain by using the Yeoman Farmer as bonus points. The number of bonus points you get is equal to the number of scoring categories for which you would otherwise have scored negative points.
Negative points that are compensated by the Yeoman Farmer are not counted as negative for the Constable K276 .
If you use the Horse K135 to compensate for a missing type of animals, you still receive a bonus point for that animal from the Yeoman Farmer (and 2 bonus points from the Horse).
The Yeoman Farmer also compensates negative (bonus) points for Forest Path M048 , Built-in Oven M044 , etc.

name: Undergardener
text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you also receive 1 vegetable.

name: Conjurer
text: Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an action space, you receive1 additional grain.
rulings: If another player uses the Juggler I237 with a "Traveling Players" action, he pays you 1 food.

name: Greengrocer
text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action space, you also receive 1 vegetable.

name: Storyteller
text: Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an action space, you can leave 1 food on the space and receive 1 vegetable instead.
rulings: If another player uses the Juggler I237 with a "Traveling Players" action, he pays you 1 food.
If you have the Dancer E212 as well, you receive at least 4 food and 1 vegetable when you leave one food on the action space.

name: Estate Manager
text: At the end of the game, you receive bonus points, if for none of the three animal species, any other player has more animals than you: in a 3-player game, 2 points; in a 4-player game, 3 points; in a 5-player game, 4 points.
rulings: If a player has the Horse K135 , it does not count as 1 animal of a missing type of his choice when counting animals for the Estate Manager. The House Goat K120 does not count as an animal.

name: Dock Worker
text: At any time, you can use the Dock Worker to convert 3 wood to either 1 clay, 1 reed or 1 stone, or to convert 2 clay, 2 reed or 2 stone to 1 other building resource.
rulings: Building resources are wood, clay, reed, and stone.

name: Chief
text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your stone house. Playing this card costs an additional 2 food.
rulings: If you have the Half-timbered House E21 as well, you use both to get 4 points per room; if you have the Mansion K144 as well, or all three of Chief, Mansion and Half-timbered House, you use them all to get 5 points per room.

name: Chief's Daughter
text: If another player plays the Chief E172 card, you can play this card immediately at no cost. At the end of the game, you receive 3 bonus points if you have a stone house, 1 if you have a clay hut.
rulings: You can also play this card using an action space in the usual way.
If you play the Chief E172 yourself, you may not play the Chief's Daughter at the same time.
In a solo game, you can only play this card using an action space.
If you have the Clogs E28 as well, you get points for both cards.

If you play the Chief's Daughter because the Chief is played, this activates the Bread Paddle K111 , Bookshelf K112 , Patron E192 , and Educator K271 , but not the Writing Desk E49 , Per- ¨ petual Student K275 , or Therapist O03 .

name: Tutor
text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each occupation that you play after this one.
rulings: You may note the bonus points on the scoring pad immediately, or you can simply lay out your occupations in the order that you play them.
The Academic E148 counts as two occupations.
If you keep this card after playing a round in a series of solo games, you also receive bonus points for permanent occupation cards kept in play after subsequent games.
(FotM) Up to a maximum of 6 bonus points.

name: Hedge Keeper
text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you can build 3 additional fences without paying any additional wood.
rulings: Is activated when you use the Fence Overseer K312 , Fence Builder I263 or Fence Deliveryman I265 , or when you play the Mini Pasture E40 .
If you also have the Farmer E160 , Stablehand E207 , Wood Carver K301 or Sawhorse K121 , you may use all of those cards during the same fence building action.

The free fences from the Hedge Keeper are built in the same fence building action as all other fences, but in an order of your choosing. However, you may not use the Hedge Keeper for the first fence you build in a turn.
Can only be used once per turn.
Only after building the extra fences, you need to follow the conditions for building fences (i.e. no open pastures).
You may choose to build fewer than 3 fences, for example, if you do not have 3 unbuilt fences left.
If you build no free fences, or fewer than 3, you cannot save the other free fences for later rounds.

name: Woodcutter
text: You receive 1 additional wood whenever you use a person's action to take wood.
rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which wood is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no wood (e.g. because of the Wood Distributor K284 ).
Is activated even if you leave all the wood on the action space because of Basket E34 , Mushroom Collector E196 , or Pig Catcher I253 .
Is activated when you use an action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game, and the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take wood.
Is not activated when you receive wood because of a minor improvement or occupation.

name: Wooden Hut Builder
text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your wooden hut.

name: Hut Builder
text: Play this card during round 1-4. At the start of round 11, you can extend your hut by 1 room at no cost, as long as you have not yet renovated to a stone house.
rulings: When the card is played, put a room tile on the round 11 action space as a reminder.
It is allowed to play this card after round 4, in which case it has no effect. It still counts as a played occupation, e.g. for the prerequisite of an improvement.
If you choose not to build a free room at the start of round 11, you cannot build it later in the game.

name: Merchant
text: Whenever you use a "minor improvement" or "minor or major improvement" action, you can pay 1 food to use the action a second time.
rulings: If you use a "major or minor improvement" action, you can either play 2 major or 2 minor improvements, or 1 major and 1 minor improvement.
Is activated by every improvement you play, even if you play several in a single turn.

Using both Merchant and Traveling Salesman K281 , you can play up to 4 minor improvements for 1 food on a "major or minor improvement" action space, or you can acquire 2 major improvements for 1 food on a "minor improvement" action space.

Using both Merchant and Businessman I228 , you can first play 1 minor improvement and 1 major or minor improvement, and then additionally for 1 food, either 2 minor improvements or 1 major and 1 minor improvement when you use the "Starting Player" action space.
If you receive goods or food from the first improvement, you may use them to pay for the use of the merchant, or for buying the second improvement.
Is activated when you use the Scholar K279 to play an improvement.

name: Hobby Farmer
text: When you play this card, you receive 1 vegetable that you may sow immediately if you have an empty plowed field.
rulings: You perform a sowing action, which you can only use for this vegetable.
Sowing the vegetable activates the Fieldsman I219 , Smallholder K286, Potato Dibber E32 , Liquid Manure K118 , Planter Box I90 and another player's Field Worker I224 .
Sowing the vegetable is optional. You may not sow anything else, including wood on Forester ¨ K278 and Copse I78 , or food on Vineyard O21 .
You may sow the vegetable on the Bean Field E18 , Lettuce Patch E47 , or Turnip Field K137 .

name: Cook
text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, only 2 of your people eat 2 food each; all others are satisfied with only 1 food each.
rulings: You still need to feed newborns with 1 food.

name: Charcoal Burner
text: You receive 1 food and 1 wood whenever any player (including you) builds a baking improvement with a "baking" symbol.
rulings: You can place 1 food and 1 wood on the affected unbuilt major improvements to remind you to take the tokens.
You also receive the food and wood when an improvement is upgraded to a baking improvement.

name: Basketmaker
text: In each harvest, the Basketmaker can convert up to 1 reed to 3 food.

name: Grocer
text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 vegetable, reed, clay, wood, vegetable, stone, grain, reed on this card. At any time, you may buy the top item for 1 food.
rulings: The resources come from the general supply, and are not part of your personal supply until you buy them.

name: Clay Firer
text: You can use the Clay Firer at any time to convert 2 clay to 1 stone or 3 clay to 2 stone.

name: Clay Seller
text: You can use the Clay Seller at any time to convert 2 clay to 1 sheep or 1 reed, 3 clay to 1 wild boar or 1 stone, and/or 4 clay to 1 cattle.
rulings: If you receive animals from the Clay Seller, you may convert them to food with an appropriate improvement without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

name: Clay Deliveryman
text: Place 1 clay on each remaining space for rounds 6 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the clay.
rulings: You do not receive clay for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

name: Clay Mixer
text: Whenever you take only clay with one of your people's actions, you receive 2 additional clay.
rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which clay is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no clay (e.g. when you have ˇ the Bureaucrat C07 ).
Is also activated by the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take clay.
Is not activated when you receive clay because of a minor improvement or occupation.
Is activated when you take clay from an action space, and at the same time receive other goods because of a minor improvement or occupation.

name: Lord of the Manor
text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each scoring category where you have scored the maximum 4 points.
rulings: The bonus point is also awarded for 4 fenced stables.
Bonus points from other cards, such as the Brewery K110 or the Schnaps Distillery I98 , are not taken into account when scoring the Lord of the Manor E189 .

name: Maid
text: Once you have built a clay hut, place 1 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.
rulings: If you already have a clay hut or a stone house when you play this card, place the food immediately.

name: Mason
text: Once during the game, at any time after your stone house reaches at least 4 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.
rulings: You may place a room tile on this card to show that you have not yet taken the extension action.
You do not have to use an action space to build a room with the Mason.

name: Patron
text: In future, whenever you play an occupation, you receive 2 food before you pay the costs of the occupation.
rulings: If you use the Writing Desk E49 , you receive food for both occupations you play.
If you also have the Bookshelf K112 , you can use both to receive 5 food for each occupation you play.

name: Pastor
text: If, when you play this card or later in the game, you are the last player to have only 2 rooms in your home, you receive 3 wood, 2 clay, 1 reed and 1 stone.
rulings: You choose the moment at which you're paid, but it has to be after the last player (other than you) build his third room and before you build your third room yourself.

name: Plough Driver
text: Once you have a stone house, you can pay 1 food at the start of each round to plough (at most) 1 field.
rulings: When you use the Plough Driver, you may not use a plough or the Harrow I68 to plough multiple fields.
Food that you receive at the start of a round (e.g. because of the Well A10 ) can be used to pay for the field immediately.

name: Plough Maker
text: Whenever you use either of the "Plough 1 Field" or "Plough Field and Sow" action spaces, you can pay 1 food to plough 1 additional field.
rulings: You may use one of the ploughs or the Harrow I68 at the same time as the Plough Maker (unlike the Plough Driver E194 ); in that case, you pay 1 food to plough 1 field more than the plough or Harrow allows.

name: Mushroom Collector
text: Whenever you use a person's action to take wood that is lying on an action space, you can leave 1 of that wood on the space and receive 2 food in exchange.
rulings: If you also have the Basket E34 , you can use both cards to leave 3 wood to receive 5 food.

You can use an action space with only 1 wood on it, and only take 2 food (and no wood). This still counts as taking wood, and activates e.g. the Berry Picker E152 , Wood Cart I79 , and Pieceworker K268 .
If there is no wood on the action space, for example because of the Wood Distributor K284 , you may not use the Mushroom Collector.
Is activated when you use an action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.
Is not activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game.

name: Braggart
text: At the end of the game, you receive bonus points for the number of improvements in front of you: for 9 or more improvements, 9 points; for 8 improvements, 7 points; for 7 improvements, 5 points; for 6 improvements, 3 points; for 5 improvements, 1 point.
rulings: Both major and minor improvements count. Discarded improvements do not.

name: Ratcatcher
text: In rounds 10 and 12, all other players may not place 1 of their family's offspring (if they have any). This card may only be played until the end of Round 9.
rulings: The idle family members do count towards the question whether there is space in the hut or house for family growth.
You yourself may place all your family members.
All family members except for the first two are offspring; not only newborns.
Guests and the occupant of the Reed Hut K138 are not offspring and may always be played.
You are allowed to play this card after round 9, but it has no effect. Remove it from the game immediately after you've played it.

name: Renovator
text: Pay 2 less clay to renovate to a clay hut, and pay 2 less stone to renovate to a stone house.

name: Conservator
text: You can renovate your wooden hut to a stone house without first needing to renovate it to a clay hut.
rulings: You may not use both the Conservator and the Stone Breaker K303 or the Conservator and the Builder's Trowel E50 to renovate your wooden hut to a stone house out of turn.
The renovation is paid, as usual, with 1 stone per room plus 1 reed, and you still have to take a "Renovate" action to use the Conservator.
Activates the Clay Hut Builder I242 when used.

name: Cattle Whisperer
text: Add 5 and 9 to the number of the current round. Place 1 cattle on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the cattle.
rulings: If this card is already in play at the start of a solo game, the current round number is 0. You receive the cattle on rounds 5 and 9.
You may immediately convert the animals into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

name: Seasonal Worker
text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you receive 1 additional grain. From round 6 onwards, you can choose to receive 1 vegetable instead.

name: Shepherd
text: During each harvest, if you have at least 4 sheep during the breeding phase, you receive 2 lambs instead of 1 as long as you have room for them.

name: Master Shepherd
text: Place 1 sheep on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the sheep.
rulings: You may immediately convert the animal into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for it in your farmyard.

name: Reed Collector
text: Place 1 reed on the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the reed.

name: Swineherd
text: Whenever you use the "1 Wild Boar" action space, you receive 1 additional wild boar from the general supply.
rulings: The "1 Wild Boar" action space is added in round 8 or 9.
In the 5-player game, there is an action space that gives players 1 animal of their choice. The Swineherd is not activated by using this space.

name: Stablehand
text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you also receive 1 stable which you must build immediately.
rulings: The stable may be built inside or outside the fenced area.
You do not need to pay any wood for the stable.
Is activated when you play the Mini Pasture E40 .
Only 1 free stable is awarded in each turn.
Is activated when you use the Fence Overseer K312 : when you build a stable, you may fence it, after which you have to build another stable. You may not then fence that second stable.
Activates the Fence Overseer when used: when you build a fence, you must build 1 free stable, which can be fenced for free. You may not use the Stablehand again to receive a second free stable.
If you have no unbuilt stables left, or if there is no place in your farmyard where it can be placed, the Stablehand has no effect.
If you use the Hedge Keeper E175 , the additional 3 fences do not activate the Stablehand a second time.

name: Stablemaster
